reasoning: Candidate is in a fundamentally different art style from the Denzi peer set. (1) Outline weight: peers use crisp 1px pixel outlines; candidate has blended, painterly edges with no clean pixel boundary. (2) Outline color: peers dark-black outlines consistent; candidate outlines dissolve into gradient shading. (3) Shadow direction: peers flat or single-drop shadow; candidate has full volumetric shading implying overhead-front light source. (4) Shadow count: peers 0-1 shadow layers; candidate has multi-layer ambient+diffuse+specular rendering. (5) Highlight intensity: peers near-zero specular; candidate has prominent white specular blobs (high-gloss sphere treatment) completely absent from peers. (6) Color temperature: candidate warm orange dominant — not inherently wrong but pairs with a completely alien palette register. (7) Detail density: candidate packs stripe patterns, gradient banding, skull/face detail, and pedestal texture into a single sprite that dwarfs peer detail count by 5-10x. (8) Proportion: candidate sprite occupies ~4x the pixel area of peers at ostensibly matching scale, indicating a resolution/resolution-class mismatch. (9) Rendering style: peers are classic flat-to-semi-flat Denzi roguelike pixel art (iconic, read at 16px); candidate is polished mobile-game style with 3D-illusion shading — incompatible aesthetic register. (10) Palette overlap: partial warm-neutral overlap but candidate's teal-blue accent and high-key orange/white have no echo in peer palette. (11) Charm level: peers are compact, iconographic, low-key cute; candidate has a flashy, high-polish mobile charm that clashes in tone. All 11 dimensions show meaningful-to-severe divergence. This sprite cannot coexist in the same UI sheet as the Denzi peers without breaking visual cohesion.
